title: lamium maculatum var. grenieri
intro: Lamium maculatum var. grenieri, commonly known as Spotted Deadnettle, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in vitamins and minerals, offering various health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Lamium maculatum var. grenieri can be prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, and it is also sometimes used in tinctures and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
